title: eCDFP
description: eCDFP – eLearnSecurity Certified Digital Forensics Professional Hands-on digital forensics training in disk, memory, and network evidence acquisition and analysis. Applied tools like Autopsy, Volatili

#### 🧠 **eCDFP – eLearnSecurity Certified Digital Forensics Professional**

The **eCDFP** is a practical certification focused entirely on **Windows digital forensics**, designed to walk you through real-world forensics workflows — from acquisition to reporting. It deepened my understanding of how modern operating systems (especially Windows) record nearly everything users and attackers do.

* * *

**📚 Key Learning Themes**

- Forensics is about answering the **5 Ws**: Who, What, When, Where, and Why.

- Windows systems store forensic artifacts in **registries, file systems, logs, and hidden caches** — and the analyst's job is to locate, interpret, and correlate them.

* * *

#### 🧪 Course Content Overview

**📁 Section 1: Introduction & Acquisition**

- Basics of digital forensics, common challenges, and workflows.

- Acquiring forensically sound images (disk and memory).

- Validating evidence integrity.

🔧 _Tools & Skills:_ FTK Imager, hashing, write blockers, live vs dead acquisition.

* * *

**📁 Section 2: File and Disk Analysis**

- File structure analysis: headers vs extensions, metadata inspection.

- Disk partitioning, FAT vs NTFS analysis, deleted file recovery.

🔧 _Tools & Skills:_ Autopsy, Hex editors, Sleuth Kit.

* * *

**📁 Section 3: System and Network Artifacts**

- **Windows forensics artifacts:**

  - Registry hives

  - Shellbags

  - LNK files

  - Prefetch, JumpLists, ShimCache, USB history

  - IE/Edge browser forensics

- **Network forensics:**

  - Identifying C2 traffic and malicious behavior

  - Distinguishing normal vs suspicious network flows

🔧 _Tools & Skills:_ Eric Zimmerman's tools, Wireshark, Registry Explorer, KAPE.

* * *

**📁 Section 4: Logs, Timelines, and Reporting**

- Using command-line tools (grep, awk, sed) for log triage.

- Timeline creation using parsed events and metadata.

- Writing structured forensic reports for stakeholders.

* * *

#### 🧪 **Exam Format**

- **24-hour practical exam** with 30 MCQs:

  - 15 theory-based questions (course content)

  - 15 questions from lab-based scenarios (via VPN)

Immediate results upon submission. Exam is manageable **if you don’t skip labs** — practical labs directly reflect exam questions.

* * *

#### 📝 **Key Takeaways**

- Mastered the **core workflow of a forensic analyst**

- Gained deep familiarity with **Windows artifacts**

- Learned to **extract evidence**, create timelines, and **present findings clearly**

- Reinforced my ability to work through real-world incident datasets

* * *

#### 🔗 **Recommended Resources**

- [CyberDefenders](https://cyberdefenders.org/labs/)

- [13Cubed (YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/user/davisrichardg)

- [Cyber5W by Ali Hadi](https://cyber5w.com/)

- [Eric Zimmerman's Tools](https://ericzimmerman.github.io/)

- [DFIR Diva](https://dfirdiva.com/)

- [start.me Forensics Resources](https://start.me/p/q6mw4Q/forensics)
